name: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a pharmaceutical company committed to discovering, developing, and commercializing novel therapies for endocrine diseases and endocrine-related tumors, with a core expertise in targeting GPCRs with small molecules. Its lead product, PALSONIFY (paltusotine), is the first once-daily, oral treatment approved by the FDA for adults with acromegaly who had an inadequate response to surgery and/or for whom surgery is not an option. The company also has a deep pipeline of over 10 disclosed programs, including late-stage investigational candidate atumelnant for congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH) and ACTH-dependent Cushing's syndrome (ADCS), and CRN09682 for SST2-expressing neuroendocrine tumors and solid tumors.
sellsTo: Patients with carcinoid syndrome (CS) associated with neuroendocrine tumors

exposures.sentence: Our lead product, PALSONIFY (paltusotine), is the first once-daily, oral treatment approved by the FDA for the treatment of adults with acromegaly who had an inadequate response to surgery and/or for whom surgery is not an option.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of PALSONIFY for acromegaly
catalysts.sentence: On September 25, 2025, the FDA approved PALSONIFY as the first and only once-daily oral somatostatin receptor ligand for the treatment of adults with acromegaly who had an inadequate response to surgery and/or for whom surgery is not an option.

catalysts.event: Commercial availability of PALSONIFY in the U.S.
catalysts.sentence: PALSONIFY became the first oral nonpeptide SST2-agonist approved by the FDA for the treatment of acromegaly in September 2025 and was commercially available in October 2025 in the U.S.
In April 2025, we received IND clearance for CRN09682, the first candidate from the NDC platform.

catalysts.event: First patient dosed in Phase 1/2 study for CRN09682
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, the first patient received CRN09682 in the dose escalation phase of a Phase 1/2 study.

catalysts.event: First patient enrolled in Phase 3 CAREFNDR study for paltusotine in CS
catalysts.sentence: The first patient was enrolled in the Phase 3 study of paltusotine for CS in November 2025.

catalysts.event: First participant randomized in Phase 3 CALM-CAH study for atumelnant in CAH
catalysts.sentence: The first participant in the CALM-CAH study was randomized in December 2025.

catalysts.event: First participant dosed in BALANCE-CAH pediatric study for atumelnant
catalysts.sentence: The first participant in the BALANCE-CAH study was dosed in January 2026.

catalysts.event: CHMP positive opinion for PALSONIFY in Europe
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the CHMP of the EMA adopted a positive opinion, recommending the marketing authorization of PALSONIFY for the medical treatment of adult patients with acromegaly.

catalysts.event: Expected initiation of Phase 2/3 EQUILIBRIUM-ADCS study for atumelnant
catalysts.sentence: We expect to initiate an operationally seamless Phase 2/3 study of atumelnant in ADCS (EQUILIBRIUM-ADCS) in the first half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 36,000 people living with acromegaly in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Our research suggests that there are approximately 36,000 people living with acromegaly in the U.S., of which 17,000 or more are undiagnosed, 7,500 are not in active follow-up for treatment, and 11,500 are actively managed.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 17,000 patients are potential candidates for treatment with atumelnant in CAH (12,000 adults, 5,000 pediatric).
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that approximately 17,000 patients are potential candidates for treatment with atumelnant, 12,000 of which are adults and 5,000 of which are pediatric.

tamClaims.claim: Over 11,000 patients with Cushing's disease in the U.S., of which approximately 5,000 are candidates for atumelnant.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate there are over 11,000 patients with Cushing's disease in the U.S., of which approximately 5,000 patients are potential candidates for treatment with atumelnant.

tamClaims.claim: 28,000-51,000 adults diagnosed with NETs are undergoing medical management; 18,000-34,000 are candidates for paltusotine in CS.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that 28,000-51,000 adults diagnosed with NETs are undergoing medical management. Of these, it is estimated that approximately 18,000-34,000 are candidates for treatment with paltusotine, if it is approved for the treatment of CS.

tamClaims.claim: 28,000 to 51,000 patients with NENs receive medical management; 11,000 to 21,000 seek tumor control and are initial addressable population for CRN09682.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that 28,000 to 51,000 patients with NENs receive medical management, of which an estimated 11,000 to 21,000 seek treatment for tumor control and represent the initial addressable population for CRN09682.

tamClaims.claim: CAH is an orphan indication with an estimated prevalence of approximately 17,000 patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: CAH is an orphan indication with an estimated prevalence of approximately 17,000 patients in the U.S., of which 12,000 are adult and 5,000 are pediatric.

tamClaims.claim: Cushing's disease is an orphan indication with a prevalence of approximately 11,000 patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Cushing s disease is an orphan indication with a prevalence of approximately 11,000 patients in the U.S.
